rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Summary Adjudication (Added For Purpose Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: (Part 2 of 2) The second issue for which Petitioner seeks summary adjudication is the return of Decedent's share of the sale proceeds for partial satisfaction of the Judgment. Summary adjudication on this issue is denied. Petitioner has not shifted his burden by showing that Respondent cannot establish that this portion of the Judgment is a money judgment. It is meant to compensate the siblings for money improperly taken by decedent from their shares of the property's equity. Although the Judgment sets out a mechanism for the money being repaid from decedent's share of the sales proceeds, those proceeds themselves are not what was wrongfully appropriated by decedent. The money decedent took was used by him for other purposes and cannot be traced directly to any particular property or fund. Under Probate Code §§ 9300 and 9301, money judgments are payable out of the estate in the course of estate administration. Neither the law nor equity compels the conclusion that a judgment based on decedent having wrongfully taken these funds is not a money judgment. Summary adjudication of the third issue, whether Respondent's otherwise unpaid right to reimbursement must be off-set by the Petitioner's judgment award, cannot be determined yet. Respondent's Petition for Reimbursement has not yet been heard or determined. The Court declines to give an advisory option on the applicability or effect of Erlich v. Sup. Ct. of Los Angeles County (1965) 63 Cal.2d 551 on the issue. Ultimate determination of how to balance the right to recover and offsets will depend on facts not all of which are yet determined, the application of relevant law, and the exercise of the Court's equitable discretion. Petitioner is directed to prepare a form of order consistent with the Court's determination and conforming to the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure section 437(g). Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, April 24, 2019.

(Part 1 of 2) The Motion for Summary Adjudication on Son Nguyen's Petition for Enforcement of Judgment is granted as to the first issue (concerning return of the remaining proceeds from the siblings' share of the real property sale proceeds) and denied as to the remaining issues. Respondent Thao Thih Lan Nguyen did not file a Separate Statement of material facts, and the facts (as opposed to conclusions) set out in Petitioner's Separate Statement will be accepted as true. As to the first issue presented in the MSA, the $66,000 that had been initially withheld from withdrawal out of the blocked account held by the estate constitutes the remaining funds belonging to Decedent's siblings from the real property sale proceeds, per the April 5, 2016 Judgment ("the Judgment"). These funds are ordered to be released to Petitioner from the blocked account. There is no question that these funds are the remainder of the siblings' share of the real property sales proceeds, were never part of decedent's estate, and are not subject to estate administration. Any estate claims Respondent may have cannot be collected from funds that do not belong to the estate, and any personal claims she may have against the siblings are not properly dealt with in the estate administration. A motion for summary adjudication may be granted only if it completely disposes of a cause of action, an affirmative defense, a claim for damages, or an issue of duty. Neither of Petitioner's other summary adjudication issues would fully resolve Petitioner's claim to recover money decedent Tim Nguyen took from his siblings' share of the real property equity, which is the gravamen of Petitioner's claim and the subject of paragraph 5 of the Judgment. Even if the issues were otherwise appropriate for summary adjudication, however, the motion would fail as to them for the following reasons.
